summary: Benchmark labels and mechanics separate curl CLI from same-process libcurl, use one reused libcurl Easy handle across warmup and measurement, record server-side trace parity plus request-shape bytes, keep phase timing opt-in, and support separate diagnostic result/figure directories. The canonical ylong_base full matrix is valid as a fair SOTA counterexample, not as a speedup claim; a tokio_base candidate full matrix is separately recorded and also refutes SOTA.

source_changes.summary: Adds optional libcurl_bench feature for benchmark-only same-process libcurl baseline support.
Links libcurl only when libcurl_bench is enabled, with optional LIBCURL_LIB_DIR for non-default library locations.

Renames the executable baseline output to curl_cli and adds YLONG_LIBCURL=1 same-process libcurl baseline using libcurl easy API with proxy TLS settings, HTTP response-code validation, per-request latency, CPU, RSS, and error statistics.
Keeps https_proxy_bench available under either ylong_base or tokio_base while still requiring async, http1_1, and OpenSSL support.

Adds --baseline curl-cli|libcurl|both, --bench-bin, --scenario, CONNECT relay to a real origin, local CA certificate generation, proxy mTLS fixture support, output parsing for curl_cli/libcurl labels, confidence-width summary columns, and scenario comparison ratios.

Rewrites benchmark wording to distinguish curl CLI from libcurl, documents the scenario-aware fair matrix, removes the old curl/libcurl conflation, and marks checked-in results as counterexample evidence rather than SOTA performance evidence.
libcurl baseline now uses a reusable Runner/Easy handle across warmup and measured requests and supports YLONG_BENCH_CLIENTS for one-client-per-process fairness tracing.
Adds server-side proxy/origin trace counters, per-client benchmark invocation, trace columns in CSV outputs, and negative speedup plotting so regressions are visible.
Adds --result-dir and --figure-dir so diagnostic phase runs do not overwrite canonical threshold artifacts.

verification_commands.observed: 10 tests passed for phase parsing, opt-in phase env, custom result dir, custom figure dir, CONNECT relay, proxy mTLS fixture, verified TLS env construction, HTTPS-origin through HTTPS proxy, and summary ratio/CI helpers.

verification_commands.command: PATH="$HOME/.cargo/bin:$PATH" CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ylong_http_target_bench_check CARGO_NET_GIT_FETCH_WITH_CLI=true GIT_CONFIG_COUNT=1 GIT_CONFIG_KEY_0='url.https://github.com/openharmony/commonlibrary_rust_ylong_runtime.git.insteadOf' GIT_CONFIG_VALUE_0='https://gitee.com/openharmony/commonlibrary_rust_ylong_runtime.git' cargo check -p ylong_http_client --bin https_proxy_bench --no-default-features --features async,http1_1,ylong_base,c_openssl_3_0,libcurl_bench

PATH="$HOME/.cargo/bin:$PATH" CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ylong_http_target_release CARGO_NET_GIT_FETCH_WITH_CLI=true GIT_CONFIG_COUNT=1 GIT_CONFIG_KEY_0='url.https://github.com/openharmony/commonlibrary_rust_ylong_runtime.git.insteadOf' GIT_CONFIG_VALUE_0='https://gitee.com/openharmony/commonlibrary_rust_ylong_runtime.git' cargo build -p ylong_http_client --release --bin https_proxy_bench --no-default-features --features async,http1_1,ylong_base,c_openssl_3_0,libcurl_bench

verification_commands.observed: http-over-https-proxy, https-over-https-proxy, and proxy-mtls-https-origin scenarios completed for repeats 1..3.
libcurl.so.4 => /lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libcurl.so.4; libssl.so.3 and libcrypto.so.3 resolved from /lib/x86_64-linux-gnu
Five-repeat all-scenario libcurl matrix completed with matching connection reuse traces; current ratios refute SOTA performance rather than proving it.

verification_commands.command: PATH="$HOME/.cargo/bin:$PATH" CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ylong_http_target_s9_reuse_check CARGO_NET_GIT_FETCH_WITH_CLI=true GIT_CONFIG_COUNT=1 GIT_CONFIG_KEY_0='url.https://github.com/openharmony/commonlibrary_rust_ylong_runtime.git.insteadOf' GIT_CONFIG_VALUE_0='https://gitee.com/openharmony/commonlibrary_rust_ylong_runtime.git' cargo check -p ylong_http_client --bin https_proxy_bench --no-default-features --features async,http1_1,ylong_base,c_openssl_3_0,libcurl_bench

PATH="$HOME/.cargo/bin:$PATH" CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ylong_http_target_proxy_tests CARGO_NET_GIT_FETCH_WITH_CLI=true GIT_CONFIG_COUNT=1 GIT_CONFIG_KEY_0='url.https://github.com/openharmony/commonlibrary_rust_ylong_runtime.git.insteadOf' GIT_CONFIG_VALUE_0='https://gitee.com/openharmony/commonlibrary_rust_ylong_runtime.git' cargo test -p ylong_http_client --test sdv_async_https_proxy_tls --no-default-features --features async,http1_1,tokio_base,c_openssl_3_0

PATH="$HOME/.cargo/bin:$PATH" CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ylong_http_target_proxy_tests CARGO_NET_GIT_FETCH_WITH_CLI=true GIT_CONFIG_COUNT=1 GIT_CONFIG_KEY_0='url.https://github.com/openharmony/commonlibrary_rust_ylong_runtime.git.insteadOf' GIT_CONFIG_VALUE_0='https://gitee.com/openharmony/commonlibrary_rust_ylong_runtime.git' cargo test -p ylong_http_client --test sdv_sync_https_proxy_tls --no-default-features --features sync,async,http1_1,tokio_base,c_openssl_3_0
verification_commands.observed: 2 tests passed. The sync-only tokio_base feature combination is not valid in this crate because runtime Semaphore/Sleep exports are gated by async; adding async is the valid test build.

verification_commands.command: PATH="$HOME/.cargo/bin:$PATH" CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ylong_http_target_proxy_no_tls CARGO_NET_GIT_FETCH_WITH_CLI=true GIT_CONFIG_COUNT=1 GIT_CONFIG_KEY_0='url.https://github.com/openharmony/commonlibrary_rust_ylong_runtime.git.insteadOf' GIT_CONFIG_VALUE_0='https://gitee.com/openharmony/commonlibrary_rust_ylong_runtime.git' cargo test -p ylong_http_client --test sdv_https_proxy_no_tls --no-default-features --features async,sync,http1_1,tokio_base

verification_commands.observed: Only nightly-only rustfmt.toml option warnings were emitted.
goal_achieved_permitted=false; S9, S10, final-report.json, and runtime counterexample review remain incomplete.

verification_commands.command: PATH="$HOME/.cargo/bin:$PATH" python3 docs/benchmarks/run_https_proxy_bench.py --baseline libcurl --scenario all --requests 200,1000,3000 --repeats 5 --warmup 50 --bench-bin /tmp/ylong_http_target_tokio_release/release/https_proxy_bench --result-dir docs/benchmarks/results/tokio-full --figure-dir docs/benchmarks/results/tokio-full
verification_commands.observed: tokio_base candidate full matrix completed with zero errors; 1.065x throughput geomean and 0.862x worst cell still refute SOTA.

fairness_boundary: curl_cli and libcurl are distinct client labels and should not be merged into curl/libcurl prose.
The checked-in three-scenario libcurl run proves the same-process baseline path, verified local CA proxy/origin TLS, proxy mTLS fixture, and metric columns are executable.
The current canonical fair matrix has at least five measured repetitions and reuse tracing, but it refutes SOTA because throughput, p50/p95, CPU, and flagship gates fail.

The current canonical fair matrix has at least five measured repetitions, reuse tracing, and request-shape byte counters, but it refutes SOTA because throughput, p50/p95, CPU, and flagship gates fail.

fairness_counterexample_resolved.old_issue: libcurl warmup and measurement previously used separate Easy handles, causing extra baseline connections.
fairness_counterexample_resolved.resolution: Runner now reuses one Easy handle; trace shows both clients use one proxy connection per repeat and one CONNECT/origin TLS connection in HTTPS-origin scenarios.

runtime_candidate_matrix.command: PATH="$HOME/.cargo/bin:$PATH" python3 docs/benchmarks/run_https_proxy_bench.py --baseline libcurl --scenario all --requests 200,1000,3000 --repeats 5 --warmup 50 --bench-bin /tmp/ylong_http_target_tokio_release/release/https_proxy_bench --result-dir docs/benchmarks/results/tokio-full --figure-dir docs/benchmarks/results/tokio-full
